Jones in the Fastlane is a pressure-management game about clawing your way out of survival mode. Every cycle you move through the city, spend limited hours, protect your sanity, and decide whether to chase stability, gamble on hustles, or invest in the long climb upward. Burn Rate is the weekly bill you must cover just to stay operational. Debt grows when you miss it. Ambient Stress is the background sanity drain that hits every cycle before your rest recovery. The run gets tighter if you ignore those systems or spend all your time reacting. The run opens up when you land better work, build education, and turn desperate short-term choices into sustainable momentum. The point is to survive the squeeze long enough to become dangerous.

Authoritative Week 2 is complete for Persona A. I recovered the lost browser session by explicitly importing the Week 1 checkpoint, advanced exactly one in-game week, exported the new Week 2 checkpoint, and corrected the stale workflow docs to match the live build.

The key finding is not the labor route, which still held at `Sanitation-T3 + Work Shift x3`, but the sanity accounting. Week 2 closed on the live summary at `₡444 / Debt ₡0 / Hunger 40% / Sanity 22`, with active `AD_FATIGUE` from `NETWORK STIMULUS DROP`. The modal’s visible sanity lines sum to `0`, its total says `-11`, and the exported checkpoint lands at `22` sanity, so there is a real summary-integrity mismatch to carry into Week 3.
